title: Protocols to examine integrin binding with Talin and protocol to test integrin activation by talin
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"rlmh5","text":"Integrin activation is an important process in cell biology. Described here are two protocols. One is a general protocol of a cell based assay to test the ability of a protein (in this case, talin) to activate integrin.  The other is a protocol to test the interaction between integrin tail and its tentative binding partner.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: On the day before transfection, plate 2~2.5 x 10^6 A5 cells on a 10cm petridish
On the day of transfection the cells should be 50% to 90% confluence.
Transfect A5 cells with the appropriate plasmids with fluorescent marker. (In this case, co-transfect 0.1μg of EGFP plasmid as the transfection marker with either talin F2F3 or pcDNA as control.)
Wait for 24 hours before FACS
Trypsin digest the cells, wash the cells with PBS buffer, and count cells.
For each experimental condition add 6 x 10^5 cells to a FACS tube
Pellet cells, 5 min 1000rpm benchtop centrifuge, at RT, decant supernatant
Resuspend cells in 50 microliter PBS buffer. Add activation specific antibody, PAC1 to assess the activation state of the integrins and incubate at room temperature for 30mins.
Add 500 microliter PBS buffer to wash the cells, then pellet the cells for 5 min at 1000rpm benchtop centrifuge. Decant supernatant.
Resuspend the cells with 50 microliter PBS buffer, add Alexa647-antiMouse IgM, incubate on ice for 30mins.
Add 1 microliter PI, incubate for another 5 mins.
Add 500 microliter PBS buffer to wash the cells, then pellet the cells for 5 min at 1000rpm benchtop centrifuge. Decant supernatant.
Resuspend the cells with 500 microliter PBS buffer and analyze the amount of PAC1 binding, which is a reflection of the integrin activation state.
Repeat the experiment with F2F3 mutant that does not bind to integrin beta3 tail.
Wash Neutravidin agarose beads 3 times with saline.
Dissolve recombinant model integrin cytoplasmic domain (biotinated) at 10mg/ml in dH2O.
Immobilize integrin cytoplasmic domains to the neutravidin beads at 0.8mg per 1ml 50% slurry beads. Incubate at 4 degree overnight.
Wash the beads twice with saline

Wash the beads three more times with the desirable integrin tail-cytoplasmic protein binding buffer. (In the case of talin, we use PN buffer consists of 20 mM PIPES, 50 mM NaCl, 150 mM Sucrose, 1mM Na3VO4, 50 mM NaF, 40 mM Na4P2O7*10H2O, 0.1% Triton X-100, pH 6.8)
Take a sample to run on SDS Page to determine the concentration of the integrin tail proteins on the beads.
Set up 700 microliter PN buffer containing recombinant talin F2F3 protein and F2F3 mutant at a protein concentration of around 20nM.
Take a sample of 20 microliter as the input control

Add 40 microliter of neutravidin slurry (50% slurry) to the 700 microliter F2F3 solution. Make sure the total amount of tail proteins are the same for different integrin tails based on the protein concentration measure in step 6.
Incubate the binding mixture at 4 degree overnight with end over end rocking.
Wash the beads for 5 times with PN buffer (by spinning down at 2000rpm for 4 mins).
Elute the bound protein with 20 ul of SDS-PAGE loading buffer and analyze the eluent by SDS-PAGE.
After the electrophoresis is finished, cut off the gel below 25K to stain for the integrin tail capturing to make sure that equal amount of integrin tails are present for the experiments.
Transfer the rest of the gel to a nitrocellulose membrane and blot the membrane for talin binding with the appropriate antibody.
Compare the amount of integrin tail binding between F2F3 and F2F3 mutants.
2 days
creator: Protocols Importer
